Member of Parliament for West Worcestershire since 2010

Served in the Government between 2014 and 2019 in the Whips Office, His Majesty’s Treasury,  the Ministry of Defence, the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office and the Department for International Development. Chair of the UK Parliamentary Delegation to the Interparliamentary Union 2019-2022 and Chair of Treasury Select Committee 2022-2024.

In Pictures
MP Welcomes Speedy Action to Protect Broadband Services
15 April 2026

West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has welcomed action which will see a telegraph pole relocated at the site of the collapsed road on the outskirts of Tenbury Wells.
MP Joins Local Opposition to Rural Sports Cull
13 April 2026

West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has slammed the Government’s plans to cull rural sports with consultations which threaten people who enjoy hunting and shooting pursuits.
MP Calls for Common Sense on Shotgun Licence Review
1 April 2026

West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has urged the Home Office to re-think the proposals to review shotgun licensing which could drive local people away from the popular sport.
News
Local Concerns Reflected in Malvern Hills Bill Amendments
29 April 2026

Legislative proposals to revise the ways that the Malvern Hills are preserved will return to the House of Lords but with a series of amendments reflecting the objections raised by local people.
Malvern Students Back Social Media Restrictions
29 April 2026

Students at a Malvern school would support measures preventing young people’s access to social media and don’t think the voting age should be changed.
MP Calls for Priority Shift to Fix County Roads
28 April 2026

West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has urged the county council to urgently re-think its highways priorities to allow for a swift fix of the collapsed road on the edge of Tenbury Wells and the final completion of the A449.
All News
In Parliament
Dame Harriett Baldwin seeks confirmation that no money will be paid to Mauritius over failed treaty
13 April 2026

Now that Parliament has not ratified the treaty on the future of the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory, Dame Harriett Baldwin seeks confirmation that no money will now be paid to the Mauritian Government.
Dame Harriett Baldwin urges PM to act to protect helium supply for NHS
13 April 2026

Speaking in a debate on the situation in the middle east and the implications for Britain’s security, Dame Harriett Baldwin raises her concerns with the PM that the UK relies on the Gulf for helium which is vital for MRI scans in the NHS.
Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts PM over 9% council tax hike
25 March 2026

Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts the Prime Minister directly over his Minister’s decision to give permission to Worcestershire County Council to hike council tax by 9%.
All Parliament News
Key Issues
The Malvern Hills Bill
Proposed amendments to the Malvern Hills Bill
The Environment Agency Tenbury Wells newsletter Mar 2026
Farmer’s lobby
Gaza
Assisted Dying Bill
Local Government Reorganisation
Hospital Trust league tables
Sign up to Dame Harriett’s newsletter
Latest from X

Local Campaigns
Transport

Dame Harriett campaigns for improved transport infrastructure, safer roads and improved rail services.
Rural Economy

A strong rural economy is vital in West Worcestershire and Dame Harriett regularly speaks up for farmers and food producers in Westminster.
Flooding

Dame Harriett continues to campaign for flood defences for Severn Stoke and Tenbury Wells. 

 

Dame Harriett Baldwin MP
House of Commons
Westminster
London, SW1A 0AA

Tel: 01684 585165

Email: harriett.baldwin.mp@parliament.uk

Contact


Malvern Hills: Alfrick and Leigh, Baldwin, Broadheath, Chase, Dyson Perrins, Hallow, Kempsey, Lindridge, Link, Longdon, Martley, Morton, Pickersleigh, Priory, Ripple, Teme Valley, Tenbury, Upton and Hanley, Wells, West, Woodbury. Wychavon: Bredon, Eckington, Elmley Castle and Somerville, Pershore
